caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The supplied 4-digit 5416 role mix is a severe proxy mismatch for 541620 and omits the actual environmental-science, geology, engineering, and field-technician core.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: S3 covers AEC firms and S6 is occupational evidence; neither measures task automation in lower-middle-market US 541620 firms.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: These are LLM/workflow estimates and do not count physical field automation as immediate labor replacement.
inputs.pi_dist: No reopened source provides a NAICS 541620 billing-mix or AI-savings pass-through measurement.
inputs.pi_dist: The selected value is a judgment across heterogeneous Phase I, permitting, remediation, monitoring, and advisory work.
inputs.pi_moat: This measures retention of savings, not the persistence of paid demand; the latter is assessed only in terminal demand.
inputs.pi_moat: Moat differs sharply by client program, geography, credentials, and subsegment.
inputs.t50_years: No representative survey measures 50% effective AI adoption for US NAICS 541620.
inputs.t50_years: The selected date excludes mere tool availability and requires material use in the labor-saving workflows scored in V.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: S2 and S3 do not publish a 541620 effective-adoption percentage; this is not a direct observation.
inputs.historical_analogs: This is an AEC analogy, not a quantitative historical adoption series for 541620.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: No reopened source provides an extracted owner-age statistic for NAICS 541620 or the $1-10M EBITDA target band.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: False means not documented by the reopened evidence, not that transition risk is absent.
inputs.active_consolidators: The count is not a verified census and the cited markets include broader environmental engineering and global ESG consulting.
inputs.buy_mult: S4 is global and broader than US environmental consulting and its reported median is not a small-target entry comp.
inputs.buy_mult: Small-firm figures require EBITDA normalization after market-rate owner compensation.
inputs.exit_mult: No reopened source reports directly comparable US 541620 entry and exit transactions.
inputs.exit_mult: The selected spread is a roll-up hypothesis, not evidence that multiple expansion will occur.
inputs.pricing_structure: No reopened public source reports a NAICS 541620 revenue split across T&M, fixed-fee, and cost-reimbursable contracts.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $237,012 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541620); class <5: 5,655 firms, avg revenue/firm $366,869 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $57,415 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 1,132 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,312,107 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $205,345 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 731 firms, avg revenue/firm $2,656,194 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $415,694 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 613 firms, avg revenue/firm $8,002,701 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,252,423 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 151 firms, avg revenue/firm $34,041,159 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $5,327,441 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 87 firms, avg revenue/firm $83,292,287 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $13,035,243 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 764

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: The fractions credit text, data, reporting, scheduling, and administrative tasks but exclude field sampling, site observation, regulator/client negotiation, and accountable professional judgment. They are constrained by BLS task descriptions and AEC workflow evidence, not measured for the supplied role mix.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541620 ($9,743,109,286) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541620 ($22,793,926,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: Mechanics pass exactly and all six score-driving sources were accessible when reopened. The sources support the useful directional thesis that environmental consulting has durable regulated and field-dependent work, meaningful document and data automation opportunities, partial capture, and active consolidation, but the score relies on openly disclosed 5416, AEC, ESG, and occupational proxies plus several unmeasured estimates. Those limitations warrant publication caveats but do not constitute a material research defect or reverse the thesis.

sources.title: All Appropriate Inquiries Rule: Definition of Environmental Professional | US EPA
sources.what_it_supports: EPA describes the qualifications and professional-judgment role of an environmental professional in the AAI rule, including education, experience, and qualifying license pathways.

sources.title: U.S. Environmental Consulting & Engineering Industry | Environmental Business Journal
sources.what_it_supports: EBJ reports current US environmental consulting and engineering growth, AI as a productivity differentiator, policy sensitivity, and an active consolidation wave. Its frame is broader than NAICS 541620.

sources.title: The Role of Artificial Intelligence in the Engineering Industry | ACEC Research Institute
sources.what_it_supports: ACEC/BST commentary supports document- and data-work use cases, high expectations for AI, and an incremental adoption path constrained by engineering regulation, contracts, culture, and liability.

sources.title: ESG Consulting M&A Report 2026 | Equiteq
sources.what_it_supports: Equiteq reports 2021-25 ESG-consulting M&A activity, PE/platform participation, fragmented acquired firms, and a global private-market median valuation benchmark; it is not a small US 541620 transaction database.

sources.title: 2025 Principals, Partners + Owners Report of AEC Firms | Zweig Group
sources.what_it_supports: Zweig's 2025 AEC report covers owner and principal demographics and ownership-transition topics, but the public product page does not disclose a 541620 owner-age statistic.

sources.title: Environmental Scientists and Specialists | Occupational Outlook Handbook | US BLS
sources.what_it_supports: BLS describes environmental-scientist tasks, fieldwork, consulting demand, and a 4% projected employment increase from 2024 to 2034; it is an occupation rather than a 541620 industry survey.

title: Environmental Consulting Services
